title: The One with the Ultimate Fighting Champion

jokes.text: Do you think there's a town in Missouri or someplace called Sample? And as you're driving into the town, there's a sign and it says: 'You're in Sample.'
jokes.setup: Joey's random musings about geography
jokes.punchline: The idea of a town literally named 'Sample' with a sign stating the obvious
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey randomness - fits his character perfectly but the execution is a bit meandering

jokes.text: It's a little crowded. Do you mind? Could you skooch? Move over just a little bit. Keep on skooching.
jokes.setup: The gang trying to fit everyone in a crowded space
jokes.punchline: The word 'skooch' and the escalating demands for space
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with a fun word choice that became quotable

jokes.text: It's a feeling you get. Like when you're in somebody else's bowling shoes? That's the one.
jokes.setup: Tim trying to explain the feeling that his wife is cheating
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific analogy to wearing someone else's bowling shoes
jokes.explanation: Clever and unexpected analogy that perfectly captures an uncomfortable feeling

jokes.text: It's oozing!
jokes.setup: Tim mentioning his wound at the table
jokes.punchline: The gross revelation that it's still oozing
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor, not particularly clever

jokes.text: I've been sleeping with your wife. You're the gynecologist?
jokes.setup: Tim suspects his wife is cheating with her gynecologist
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Doug himself is the gynecologist
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the earlier setup with perfect economy and surprise

jokes.text: So what were you gonna tell us? I have no idea.
jokes.setup: After the dramatic restaurant confrontation
jokes.punchline: Monica completely forgetting what she wanted to say
jokes.explanation: Good use of anticlimax after the dramatic restaurant scene

jokes.text: Oh, keep your name. Don't take his name! Then definitely don't take his name!
jokes.setup: Phoebe advising Monica about marriage
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the name advice when there's no marriage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's persistence with bad advice

jokes.text: You can bite and pull people's hair and stuff? Anything goes except eye-gouging and fish-hooking.
jokes.setup: Describing the brutal nature of Ultimate Fighting
jokes.punchline: The specific exceptions to 'anything goes'
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between 'anything goes' and specific forbidden moves

jokes.text: Thanks. That would have been hard to describe.
jokes.setup: Someone demonstrating fish-hooking instead of explaining it
jokes.punchline: Monica's relief at not having to describe something gross
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke acknowledging the visual nature of the gag

jokes.text: My hands are totally clean. I just gave the duck a bath.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about a strange taste
jokes.punchline: Joey's bizarre logic that bathing a duck makes his hands clean
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with confidence

jokes.text: There is no I in 'team.' Yes, but there's two in 'martini.' So, everybody, back to my office.
jokes.setup: Doug's motivational team speech cliché
jokes.punchline: Chandler's clever wordplay subverting the message
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly subverts a corporate cliché - classic Chandler wit

jokes.text: This team's about hard work, but it's also about having fun. Good to have you aboard, Bing!
jokes.setup: Chandler worrying about making jokes at work
jokes.punchline: The boss loving his humor and promoting him
jokes.explanation: Predictable resolution but sets up the butt-slapping subplot

jokes.text: My boss keeps slapping my butt and acting like it's no big deal. I didn't do anything. I didn't want to be the guy who has a problem with his boss slapping his bottom.
jokes.setup: Chandler's workplace harassment situation
jokes.punchline: His awkward reasoning for not addressing it
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the subplot with Chandler's typical overthinking

jokes.text: I would never congratulate Monica on a great stew by, you know, grabbing her boob.
jokes.setup: Discussing the inappropriateness of the butt-slapping
jokes.punchline: Ross's analogy using inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Solid analogy that makes the point clearly and humorously

jokes.text: For a really great stew, you'd just stick your head in between them.
jokes.setup: Ross's boob-grabbing analogy
jokes.punchline: Joey escalating to an even more inappropriate action
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey escalation with his food obsession and lack of boundaries

jokes.text: Anything but stew.
jokes.setup: The previous stew conversation
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical response to avoid the topic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback that closes the bit

jokes.text: What could you rub on your butt that would smell bad?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's suggestion to put something smelly on Chandler's butt
jokes.punchline: Joey's innocent but gross question
jokes.explanation: Joey's innocent curiosity about something gross - in character but not brilliant

jokes.text: What if Joey was President?
jokes.setup: Joey's previous question about butt-rubbing substances
jokes.punchline: Chandler imagining the chaos of President Joey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non-sequitur that perfectly captures Chandler's horror at Joey's thought process

jokes.text: Average height, medium build, bald... Oh!
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing Bonnie to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's shocked realization about who Bonnie is
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the bald reveal, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: A housepainter! He used to be a housepainter!
jokes.setup: Pete saying Hoshi was a paid assassin
jokes.punchline: The correction that he was actually a housepainter
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that deflates Pete's attempt to sound intimidating

jokes.text: No boom-boom before big fight! How about just a boom?
jokes.setup: Hoshi forbidding sex before the fight
jokes.punchline: Pete's attempt to negotiate for partial action
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay that fits Pete's negotiating personality

jokes.text: I just bought a pretzel from one of the kids from Fame.
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on something depressing
jokes.punchline: The fallen child star selling pretzels
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny observation about child star trajectories

jokes.text: I just started wearing bras again.
jokes.setup: Ross noting Bonnie looks different
jokes.punchline: Bonnie's casual mention of bra-wearing as the change
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that delays the hair reveal while adding awkwardness

jokes.text: That girl has got hair all over her head!
jokes.setup: Rachel's panic about Bonnie having hair
jokes.punchline: The overly dramatic description of normal hair
jokes.explanation: Rachel's dramatic overreaction to the most normal thing - having hair

jokes.text: Not on purpose. He ricocheted off you and got me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the boss's butt-slapping favoritism
jokes.punchline: The physics of ricocheted butt-slapping
jokes.explanation: Absurd but logical explanation for accidental butt-slapping

jokes.text: Dartmouth? Who went to Dartmouth? Dartmouth sucks! You go to Dartmouth? No, sir. There you go! Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Doug's aggressive team building
jokes.punchline: The rapid escalation and deflation of Dartmouth hatred
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Doug's erratic management style

jokes.text: I saved 30 cents. Plus I get to keep the cup. Yay!
jokes.setup: Ross buying the Ultimate Fighting combo meal
jokes.punchline: His pathetic excitement over minimal savings and a cheap cup
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's practical nature and misplaced enthusiasm

jokes.text: It would be nice after hearing 20,000 people chant, 'You suck!'
jokes.setup: Monica trying to be supportive after Pete's fight
jokes.punchline: Pete's matter-of-fact description of the crowd's reaction
jokes.explanation: Good understatement that highlights how badly the fight went

jokes.text: There was MOSS 2 that would only schedule appointments in January.
jokes.setup: Pete's motivational story about persistence
jokes.punchline: The bizarrely specific failure of MOSS 2
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific software failure that fits Pete's tech background

jokes.text: Apparently, he trains by going to Iran and pulling the arms off thieves.
jokes.setup: Describing Pete's next opponent
jokes.punchline: The absurdly extreme training method
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny escalation of how tough the opponent is

jokes.text: I got tanked myself last night. Pretty dicey drive home. Tappan Zee Bridge never looked smaller.
jokes.setup: Doug responding to Chandler's confession about not doing work
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to drunk driving with specific details
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Doug's casual attitude toward dangerous behavior

jokes.text: Is it the swearing? Is it the constant swearing? Because I gotta tell you, if it is, you can just kiss my ass!
jokes.setup: Chandler diplomatically addressing the butt-slapping
jokes.punchline: Doug completely misunderstanding while demonstrating the swearing
jokes.explanation: Good irony as Doug demonstrates the swearing while asking about it

jokes.text: I have a rather sensitive posterior.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to address the butt-slapping diplomatically
jokes.punchline: The overly formal description of his butt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler-esque euphemism for a sensitive butt

jokes.text: Would you rather live in the shirt pocket of a sweaty giant or inside his shower drain?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's random philosophical question
jokes.punchline: The bizarrely specific and gross choices
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe weirdness with vivid, uncomfortable imagery

jokes.text: She's very sexually aggressive.
jokes.setup: Rachel panicking about Bonnie touching Ross's thigh
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact description that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's typical bluntness that escalates Rachel's worry

jokes.text: You owe me one bald girl!
jokes.setup: Rachel's panic about Ross dating Bonnie
jokes.punchline: The specific demand for a bald replacement
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd demand that captures Rachel's desperation

jokes.text: We killed them all!
jokes.setup: Looking at the ants they were watching work as a team
jokes.punchline: The sudden realization of ant genocide
jokes.explanation: Dark turn from admiring teamwork to realizing destruction

jokes.text: The day will come when children will argue over who will win a fight, me or Superman. Not that I could beat Superman, but kids are stupid.
jokes.setup: Pete's grandiose vision of his fighting success
jokes.punchline: The immediate deflation with the Superman qualifier and kid insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and deflation with perfect character logic

jokes.text: Could you leave a note? I'm on painkillers and I might not remember this tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Monica's dramatic breakup speech
jokes.punchline: Pete's practical concern about drug-induced memory loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with character-appropriate practicality

jokes.text: Of course you did. Forgot something else too, you bastard!
jokes.setup: Chandler's excuse about forgetting his briefcase
jokes.punchline: Doug's accusation followed by the butt slap
jokes.explanation: Functional callback but predictable resolution to the subplot

jokes.text: Check it out! Pete's winning! Really? No!
jokes.setup: Joey's excited announcement about Pete winning
jokes.punchline: The immediate deflation when Monica asks for confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on false hope

jokes.text: If that's his favorite area, why is he being so mean to it?
jokes.setup: Announcer saying Bruiser is going for his 'favorite area'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's innocent misunderstanding of fighting terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic applied to fighting - innocent but twisted

jokes.text: This is ironic. Your last boyfriend, Richard, didn't want to have kids. And from the looks of it, now Pete can't.
jokes.setup: Pete getting beaten in his 'favorite area'
jokes.punchline: Ross's observation about the reproductive implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark callback that connects storylines with perfect timing and delivery

tmdb_overview: To Monica's horror, Pete wants to be the Ultimate Fighting Champion. Chandler has a problem when his new boss is slaphappy.
